source.title: Circulating tumour DNA dynamics predict recurrence in stage III melanoma patients receiving neoadjuvant immunotherapy.

finding.description: Post-surgery ctDNA positivity was 100% predictive of recurrence.
finding.effect_summary: Post-surgery ctDNA positivity accurately predicts recurrence.

source_quote: Post-surgery ctDNA positivity and zero-conversion are highly predictive of recurrence, offering a window for personalised modification of adjuvant therapy.

source.title: Circulating tumour DNA dynamics predict recurrence in stage III melanoma patients receiving neoadjuvant immunotherapy.

finding.description: Median RFS was 8.0 months in patients with detectable ctDNA post-surgery compared with median not reached in patients with undetectable ctDNA.
finding.effect_summary: Detectable ctDNA post-surgery is associated with shorter RFS.

source_quote: Median RFS was 8.0 months in patients with detectable ctDNA post-surgery compared with median not reached in patients with undetectable ctDNA [HR 10.8 (95% CI 0.8-142.4); p < 0.0001].

source.title: Circulating tumour DNA dynamics predict recurrence in stage III melanoma patients receiving neoadjuvant immunotherapy.

finding.description: Median MSS was 43.0 months for patients who were positive for ctDNA post-surgery compared with median not reached for those with undetectable ctDNA.
finding.effect_summary: Detectable ctDNA post-surgery is associated with shorter MSS.

source_quote: Median MSS was 43.0 months for patients who were positive for ctDNA post-surgery compared with median not reached (approximately 4.5 year follow up) for those with undetectable ctDNA [HR 8.0 (95% CI 0.4–182.0); p = 0.0124].

source.title: Circulating tumour DNA dynamics predict recurrence in stage III melanoma patients receiving neoadjuvant immunotherapy.

finding.description: For patients with detectable pre-treatment ctDNA, the sensitivity and specificity of ctDNA post-surgery in predicting recurrence were both 100%.
finding.effect_summary: High sensitivity and specificity of ctDNA post-surgery for recurrence prediction in patients with detectable pre-treatment ctDNA.

source_quote: Thus, when pre-treatment ctDNA was detectable, the sensitivity and specificity of ctDNA post-surgery in predicting recurrence were both 1.000 (95% CI: 0.1777–1.000 and 0.8157–1.000, respectively).
